steps.instruction: Like terms have the same variable raised to the same power.
steps.work: Add (3x² + 5x − 2) + (x² − 3x + 7). Like terms: 3x² and x², 5x and −3x, −2 and 7.
steps.explanation: You can only combine terms with exactly matching variable parts.

steps.instruction: Add the coefficients of each group of like terms.
steps.explanation: Only the coefficients change — the variable parts stay the same.

steps.instruction: Combine into one polynomial in standard form.
steps.explanation: The sum of two polynomials is always a polynomial.

steps.instruction: Subtract (5x² + 2x − 3) − (2x² − 4x + 1). First distribute the minus sign to every term in the second polynomial.
steps.explanation: Subtraction means changing every sign in the second polynomial.
After distributing the negative, it becomes an addition problem.
Always double-check your signs when subtracting polynomials.
